calculatedValues.name: cvProcedureType
calculatedValues.expression: iif({ProcedureType} contains 'TIEBACK', 'Treatment: TIEBACK', iif({ProcedureType} contains 'BVEAP', 'Treatment: BVEAP', iif({ProcedureType} contains 'STENT', 'Treatment: STENT', iif({ProcedureType} contains 'ARYTENOIDECTOMY', 'Treatment: ARYTENOIDECTOMY', iif({ProcedureType} contains 'VENTRILOCORDECTOMY', 'Treatment: VENTRILOCORDECTOMY', ''))

calculatedValues.name: cvProcedureTypeShort
calculatedValues.expression: iif({ProcedureType} contains 'TIEBACK', 'tieback', iif({ProcedureType} contains 'BVEAP', 'BVEAP', iif({ProcedureType} contains 'STENT', 'stent', iif({ProcedureType} contains 'ARYTENOIDECTOMY', 'arytenoidectomy', iif({ProcedureType} contains 'VENTRILOCORDECTOMY', 'ventrilocordectomy', ''))

calculatedValues.name: cvObserveFirstPersonPast
calculatedValues.expression: iif({LifeStatus} = true, 'did you observe', 'have you observed')

calculatedValues.name: cvEndOfLife
calculatedValues.expression: iif({LifeStatus} = true, 'towards the end of {genderObjectPronoun} life', 'in {genderObjectPronoun} current state of health')

elements.name: LandingTitleHead
elements.html:
LP/GOLPP Survey 2026

Powered by LarParLife.org

elements.name: LandingVersion
elements.html: Version 0.9/26.03.27.01

elements.name: LandingWelcomeHeading
elements.html: Welcome to the LP/GOLPP Survey 2026

elements.name: LandingWelcomeText1
elements.html: This survey is the first phase of a longitudinal study designed to collect information directly from the people whose beloved companions suffer from canine laryngeal paralysis.

elements.name: LandingWelcomeText2
elements.html: By sharing what you’ve seen — symptoms, treatments, and day-to-day changes — you can help build a clearer understanding of how this disease progresses and what actually helps. Each response adds to a growing body of real-world knowledge, helping to identify patterns, compare outcomes, and move toward better answers for animals with LP and the people who care for them.

elements.name: LandingAboutPrivacyHeading
elements.html: About your privacy and personal information

elements.name: LandingAboutPrivacyText01
elements.html: We only need four pieces of information to verify survey results:

elements.name: LandingAboutPrivacyOrderedList
elements.html:
• Your first name
  • Your companion animal's name
  • The country in which you live
  • Your email address or Facebook account name

elements.name: LandingPrivacyLink
elements.html: Learn more about our Privacy Policy

elements.name: LandingAboutPrivacyText02
elements.html: This information ensures that all the survey respondents are unique and verifiable without making them identifiable outside the context of the survey.

elements.name: LandingAboutConsentHeading
elements.html: About our Consent Policy

elements.name: LandingConsentText
elements.html: By selecting 'Yes' below, you confirm that you agree to the terms of our Consent Policy.

elements.name: LandingConsentLink
elements.html: Learn more about our Consent Policy

elements.name: LandingConsent
elements.title: Do you want to proceed with the survey in accordance with our Consent Policy?

elements.name: UserInfoHead
elements.html: Before You Begin

elements.name: UserInfoText1
elements.html: If you're still grieving from the loss of your Lar Par companion, maybe it's best to do the survey later when you've had time to heal.

elements.name: UserInfoText2
elements.html: If you'd like to continue now, please tell us your LP companion's name and if they have crossed over the Rainbow Bridge.

elements.name: CmpnName
elements.title: Please tell us your companion's name:

elements.name: LifeStatus
elements.title: Has {CmpnName} crossed over the Rainbow Bridge?

elements.name: UserInfoHeading
elements.html: About You

elements.name: UserInfoText
elements.html: We're asking for your first name, which country you live in, and some optional demographics to help us understand the people who are making choices for their beloved LP companions. We also ask for contact info to prevent fake respondents and make sure each respondent is unique and verifiable.

elements.name: UserInfoVerificationText
elements.html:
We contact a random sample of respondents for verification purposes. You may or may not be contacted. You can provide an email address or, if you're more comfortable communicating via Facebook Messenger, you can provide your Facebook account name.

With your consent, we may also contact you to talk in more detail about your experience with LP.

elements.name: UserInfoContactType
elements.title: Would you like to use email or Facebook Messenger for verification?
Email address:
elements.name: UserInfoContactTypeEmail
UserInfoContactTypeFacebookInstructions
elements.title: z
elements.html: Please go to your Facebook account page and copy the URL, then paste it below. Example: https://facebook.com/my.user.name.

elements.title: Facebook account:
elements.name: UserInfoContactTypeFacebook

validators.text: Please enter a valid Facebook profile URL, e.g., https://facebook.com/your.username

elements.name: UserInfoContactOK
elements.title: May we contact you to talk more about your LP experience?

elements.name: CmpnInfoHeading
elements.html: <h2>About Your LP/GOLPP companion<h2>

elements.name: CmpnInfoText
elements.html: This section asks basic questions about your companion's species, age, breed, weight, and demeanor. The answers are required to continue with the survey. If you don't know exact information, it's OK to guesstimate.

elements.name: FactorsHeading
elements.html: Your LP/GOLPP Decisions

elements.name: FactorsText
elements.html: This section asks some questions about how you learned about LP/GOLPP and about your decision-making process. Please don‘t overthink. In this section it‘s best to answer according to your first impulse.

elements.name: FactorsPriorKnowledge
elements.title: Had you ever heard of LP before {CmpnName} got it?

elements.name: FactorsSourcesTypes
elements.title: Which sources of information have you accessed to learn about LP?

elements.name: FactorsBestSource
elements.title: Which of those information sources did you find MOST useful (please select one)?

elements.name: FactorsClearAnswers
elements.title: Did you find it hard to get clear answers to your questions so you could make informed choices for {CmpnName}?
elements.description: 1 = No, not hard at all, 5 = yes, very hard

elements.name: FactorsCostPanel
elements.title: How have financial considerations factored into your LP/GOLPP decisions?

elements.name: FactorsProcedureCost
elements.title: Cost of surgical/stent procedure:
elements.description: 1 = less important, 5 = more important

elements.name: FactorsComplicationsCost
elements.title: Added cost of possible post-op complications such as aspiration pneumonia:
elements.description: 1 = less important, 5 = more important

elements.name: FactorsSymptomsPanel
elements.title: Which aspects of {CmpnName}'s LP/GOLPP symptoms have factored into your decisions?

elements.name: FactorsSymptomsBreathingCrisis
elements.title: Fear of a breathing crisis or emergency:
elements.description: 1 = less important, 5 = more important

elements.name: FactorsSymptomsNeuropathy
elements.title: Progression of {CmpnName}'s hind-end weakness and polyneuropathy:
elements.description: 1 = less important, 5 = more important

elements.name: FactorsSurgeryRisksPanel
elements.title: Which risk factors of surgery have factored into your LP/GOLPP decisions?

elements.name: FactorsSurgeryAnesthesia
elements.title: Extended anesthesia:
elements.description: 1 = less important, 5 = more important

elements.name: FactorsSurgeryInpatient
elements.title: Risk of direct inpatient complications of surgery:
elements.description: 1 = less important, 5 = more important

elements.name: FactorsSurgeryLongTerm
elements.title: Risks of long-term complications such as aspiration pneumonia, suture failure, or infection:
elements.description: 1 = less important, 5 = more important

elements.name: FactorsGeneralPanel
elements.title: How have {CmpnName}'s age and general health considerations factored into your LP/GOLPP decisions?

elements.name: FactorsGeneralAge
elements.title: {CmpnName}'s age:
elements.description: 1 = less important, 5 = more important

elements.name: FactorsGeneralHealth
elements.title: {CmpnName}'s overall health and prospects for quality of life:
elements.description: 1 = less important, 5 = more important

elements.name: FactorsGeneralCoexisting
elements.title: {CmpnName}'s preexisting conditions other than LP/GOLPP:
elements.description: 1 = less important, 5 = more important

elements.name: FactorsCircumstancesPanel
elements.title: How have your personal circumstances factored into your decision?

elements.name: FactorsCircumstancesAccess
elements.title: Distance to or access to a qualified specialist:
elements.description: 1 = less important, 5 = more important

elements.name: FactorsCircumstancesFutureCare
elements.title: Ability to provide adequate care in the future:
elements.description: 1 = less important, 5 = more important

elements.name: FactorsOtherPanel
elements.title: What other considerations, if any, have factored into your LP/GOLPP decisions?

elements.name: FactorsOther
elements.title: Other considerations:
elements.description: Maximum 250 characters.

elements.name: SymptomsText
elements.html: This section asks questions about the early phase of your LP/GOLPP journey and how you came to believe {CmpnName} might have it.

elements.title: Years

elements.title: Does/Did {CmpnName} have a history of pulling on a neck restraint or collar?

elements.title: Had {CmpnName} been intubated under anesthesia for any reason in the 12 months prior to the first LP symptoms?

elements.name: PrimaryHeading
elements.html: Your Primary Veterinarian and {CmpnName}'s LP/GOLPP

elements.name: PrimaryText
elements.html: This section asks questions about how your primary veterinarian(s) handled {CmpnName}'s LP/GOLPP symptoms.

elements.name: PrimaryInflammation
elements.title: Did your current primary veterinarian observe any inflammation, irritation, or infection in the larynx/pharynx area?

elements.name: PrimaryReflux
elements.title: Did your current primary veterinarian mention reflux (GERD) or esophageal dysfunction in relation to {CmpnName}'s symptoms?

elements.name: PrimaryDidDiscuss
elements.title: Did your current primary vet discuss LP/GOLPP as a possible cause of {CmpnName}'s symptoms?

choices.text: No, did not mention LP/GOLPP at all
Said {CmpnName}'s symptoms were possibly due to LP/GOLPP
Said {CmpnName}'s symptoms were definitely due to LP/GOLPP

elements.name: PrimaryInfoDetails
elements.title: Did your current primary veterinarian give you useful information about LP/GOLPP?

choices.text: No, I received little or no information.
They gave me a general description of LP/GOLPP without going into detail.
Explained LP/GOLPP in detail and told me about possible options, including surgery.

elements.name: PrimaryDidRefer
elements.title: Did your primary veterinarian refer you to a specialist about {CmpnName}'s LP/GOLPP?

elements.name: PrimaryReferral
elements.title: Which specialist were you referred to?

elements.name: PrimarySurgery
elements.title: Did your primary veterinarian make a recommendation about tieback or other LP/GOLPP surgeries for {CmpnName}'s LP/GOLPP?

choices.text: Encouraged me to talk to a surgeon

elements.name: PrimarySurgeryPerception
elements.title: Do you feel like your primary veterinarian's recommendation against surgery was due to {CmpnName}'s condition or to a generally negative perception of tieback surgery?

elements.name: PrimaryChangedVets
elements.title: Did you ever change primary veterinarians because how they handled {CmpnName}'s LP/GOLPP symptoms?

elements.name: PrimaryChangedVetsReason
elements.title: What did the previous primary vet say or do that caused you to change to a different veterinarian?
elements.description: Please check all that apply.

choices.text: Said nothing could be done about {CmpnName}'s symptoms.
Couldn't give possible causes for {CmpnName}'s symptoms.
Said {CmpnName}'s symptoms were just due to age.
Ordered tests that I didn't think were relevant.

elements.name: PrimaryConfidence
elements.title: How confident have you been in your current primary veterinarian's knowledge level regarding LP/GOLPP?
elements.description: 1 = not at all confident, 5 = very confident

elements.name: ProcedureArytenoidectomyPanel

elements.description: Partial arytenoidectomy is the surgical removal of a section of one of the arytenoid (laryngeal) cartilages.

elements.description: Please choose all that apply.

elements.description: This technique is called 'ablation'.

elements.name: ProcedureAntibiotics
elements.title: Did the surgeon/stent provider give you antibiotics to prevent aspiration pneumonia after the surgery?

elements.name: ProcedureRefluxMeds
elements.title: Did the surgeon/stent provider give you medications to reflux after the surgery?

elements.name: ProcedureCost
elements.title: What was the approximate cost of the {cvProcedureTypeShort} procedure, not including post-operative complications?
elements.description: Please provide the pre-insurance cost, not the out-of-pocket cost.

elements.name: ProcedureComplications
elements.title: Were there immediate post-operative complications that required at least one extra night of inpatient care?
elements.description: Please provide the pre-insurance cost, not the out-of-pocket cost.

elements.name: ProcedureCostComplications
elements.title: What was the approximate cost of the post-operative complications, not including aspiration pneumonia?
elements.description: Please provide the pre-insurance cost, not the out-of-pocket cost.

elements.name: ProcedureComparison
elements.title: Did the surgeon explain the pros and cons of this surgery type in comparison to tieback or other LP surgery or stent procedures?

elements.name: ProcedureChoice
elements.title: Did the surgeon offer you the choice between this surgery type other LP surgery or stent procedures?

elements.name: ProcedureSurgeonInformation
elements.title: How satisfied were you with the information the surgeon provided to you about the risks and possible complications of the procedure??
elements.description: 1 = very unsatisfied, 5 = very satisfied

elements.name: ProcedureSatisfaction
elements.title: How satisfied were you with the results of the procedure?
elements.description: 1 = very unsatisfied, 5 = very satisfied

elements.name: TherapyHeading
elements.html: Therapy for LP/GOLPP

elements.name: TherapyText
elements.html: This section asks about non-traditional treatments or therapy {CmpnName} has had for LP/GOLPP.

elements.name: TherapyDidHave
elements.title: Did {CmpnName} receive acupuncture or other therapies to help with LP/GOLPP symptoms?

elements.name: TherapyType
elements.title: What therapies or treatments did {CmpnName} receive?

choices.text: Cold laser or red light therapy

elements.name: TherapyAcupunctureRatingBreathing
elements.title: Please rate the effect of acupuncture on {CmpnName}'s breathing:
elements.description: 1 = very negative, 3 = no effect,  5 = very positive

elements.name: TherapyAcupunctureRatingMobility
elements.title: Please rate the effect of acupuncture on {CmpnName}'s mobility or behavior:
elements.description: 1 = very negative, 3 = no effect,  5 = very positive

elements.name: TherapyTherapyRatingBreathing

elements.description: 1 = very negative, 3 = no effect,  5 = very positive

elements.name: TherapyTherapyRatingMobility
elements.title: Please rate the effect of physical or hydro therapy on {CmpnName}'s mobility or behavior:
elements.description: 1 = very negative, 3 = no effect,  5 = very positive

elements.name: TherapyColdLaserRatingBreathing
elements.title: How effective was cold laser or red light therapy on {CmpnName}'s breathing:
elements.description: 1 = very negative, 3 = no effect,  5 = very positive

elements.name: TherapyColdLaserRatingMobility
elements.title: Please rate the effect of cold laser or red light therapy on {CmpnName}'s mobility or behavior:
elements.description: 1 = very negative, 3 = no effect,  5 = very positive

elements.name: TherapyChiroRatingBreathing
elements.title: Please rate the effect of chiropractic on {CmpnName}'s breathing on {CmpnName}'s mobility or behavior:
elements.description: 1 = very negative, 3 = no effect,  5 = very positive

elements.name: TherapyChiroRatingMobility
elements.title: Please rate the effect of chiropractic on {CmpnName}'s mobility or behavior:
elements.description: 1 = very negative, 3 = no effect,  5 = very positive

elements.name: TherapyCost
elements.title: Average monthly cost of {CmpnName}'s acupuncture or other therapies:
elements.description: If you don't know exactly, it's OK to guesstimate.

elements.name: BreathingHeading
elements.html: Breathing Crises and Aspiration Pneumonia

elements.name: BreathingText
elements.html: This section asks questions about {CmpnName}'s experience with breathing crises and aspiration pneumonia.

elements.name: BreathingInfoLink
elements.html: What is aspiration pneumonia and why is it a frequent complication of LP/GOLPP ?

elements.name: BreathingCrisisDidHave
elements.title: Did {CmpnName} ever have a breathing crisis that caused you to fear for {cvGenderObjectPronoun} life?

elements.name: BreathingCrisisCount
elements.title: How many such breathing crises did {CmpnName} have?

elements.name: BreathingEmergencyDidHave
elements.title: Did {CmpnName} ever have a breathing emergency that required a visit to a veterinary professional?

elements.name: BreathingEmergencyCount
elements.title: How many visits did {CmpnName} make to veterinary professionals because of a breathing crisis?

elements.name: BreathingEmergencyCost
elements.title: About what was the total pre-insurance cost of these visits?

choices.text: Don't know or prefer not to say

elements.name: BreathingCrisisAdvice
elements.title: Did a veterinary professional give you any advice on how to deal with an LP-induced breathing emergency/crisis?

elements.name: BreathingCrisisAdviceType
elements.title: What advice did they give you to help you deal with a breathing crisis?

choices.text: Get them to a cool place
Apply ice, a cooling vest, or some other cooling agent
Bring them to an emergency facility immediately

elements.name: BreathingCrisisProductRecommended
elements.title: Did a veterinary professional recommend or provide you with a substance or product to deal with a breathing crisis?

elements.name: BreathingCrisisProductRecommendedType
elements.title: What substance or product did they provide or recommend?

elements.name: BreathingCrisisProductRecommendedForm
elements.title: In what form was the sedative or steroid provided?

elements.name: BreathingOxygenDidUse
elements.title: Did you ever use a supplemental oxygen product for {CmpnName}?

elements.name: BreathingCrisisOxygenPrevent
elements.title: Did the supplemental oxygen help prevent a breathing crisis?
elements.description: 1 - did not help at all,  3 = helped somewhat, 5 = helped very much

elements.name: BreathingCrisisOxygenRevive
elements.title: Did the supplemental oxygen help revive {CmpnName} from a breathing crisis?
elements.description: 1 - did not help at all, 3 = helped somewhat, 5 = helped very much

elements.name: BreathingAspirationDidHave
elements.title: Did {CmpnName} ever get aspiration pneumonia (AP)?

elements.name: BreathingAspirationCount
elements.title: How many times did {CmpnName} get AP?

elements.name: BreathingAspirationAfterProcedure
elements.title: How many times did {CmpnName} get aspiration pneumonia after the surgery or stent procedure?

elements.name: BreathingAspirationInpatient
elements.title: Did {CmpnName} ever need inpatient, overnight, or emergency care for AP?

elements.name: BreathingAspirationInpatientCount
elements.title: How many times did {CmpnName} require inpatient, overnight, or emergency care for AP?

elements.name: BreathingAspirationCost
elements.title: What was the approximate pre-insurance cost of the AP treatment for {CmpnName}:

elements.name: NeuropathyHeading
elements.html: Hind-end weakness and polyneuropathy

elements.name: NeuropathyText
elements.html: This section asks questions about whether {CmpnName} showed symptoms of polyneuropathy and how these symptoms progressed over time.

elements.name: NeuropathyText
elements.html: NOTE: Polyneuropathy refers to the weakening of motor function due to neurological degeneration, particularly in the hind end. Symptoms can include hind-end weakness and incontinence. Younger dogs with idiopathic LP often don't suffer from LP-related polyneuropathy.

elements.name: NeuropathySymptomsDoesHave
elements.title: Over the course of {CmpnName}'s LP/GOLPP journey, did {CmpnName} experience persistent hind-end weakness?

elements.name: NeuropathySymptomsFirst
elements.title: Which LP/GOLPP symptom appeared first in {CmpnName}?

choices.text: They both appeared at about the same time

elements.name: NeuropathySymptomsBreathingOnset
elements.title: About how long after you first noticed hind-end weakness did the breathing issues begin?

elements.name: NeuropathySymptomsHindEndOnset
elements.title: About now long after you first noticed breathing issues did the hind-end weakness begin?

elements.name: NeuropathySymptomsTypeProgression
elements.title: Which progressed faster, the breathing issues or the hind-end weakness?

choices.text: They both progressed at about the same rate

elements.name: NeuropathyOtherIssues
elements.title: Over the course of {CmpnName}'s LP/GOLPP, did any of these issues also appear?

choices.text: No, {CmpnName} never had any of these issues

elements.name: ConclusionHeading
elements.html: Conclusion

elements.name: Conclusion
elements.html: The last section of this survey references your earlier responses to determine how {CmpnName}'s LP/GOLPP developed over time and what factors may have played a role in the LP/GOLPP progression.

elements.name: ConclusionsPanelLifeStatusTrueText
elements.html: NOTE: These questions ask about the last station of {CmpnName}'s LP/GOLPP journey before {cvGenderSubjectPronoun} crossed over the Rainbow Bridge. If it's too painful to revisit this stage of {CmpnName}'s life, it might be best to discontinue the survey and return to it later.

elements.name: ConclusionsPanelLifeStatusFalseText
elements.html: IMPORTANT: Please answer the questions about {CmpnName} in {cvGenderObjectPronoun} current state of health:

elements.name: ConclusionSpecialConsiderations
elements.title: Please describe any special considerations for {CmpnName} due to LP-related breathing issues:

choices.text: Type, amount or frequency of feeding
Other behavior restrictions i.e., anxiety, excitement

elements.name: ConclusionGeneralCondition
elements.title: Please describe {CmpnName}'s general physical condition:

elements.name: ConclusionQOL
elements.title: How would you rate {CmpnName}'s quality of life at this stage?
elements.description: 1 = very poor, 3 = fair, 5 = very good

elements.name: ConclusionOtherConditionsNew
elements.title: Did {CmpnName} develop any new conditions during {cvGenderObjectPronoun} LP/GOLPP journey that {cvGenderSubjectPronoun} didn't have before?

elements.name: ConclusionOtherConditions
elements.title: What other conditions did {CmpnName} develop during {cvGenderObjectPronoun} LP/GOLPP journey?
elements.description: The selections you made about the beginning of {CmpnName}'s LP/GOLPP journey are preselected.

elements.name: ConclusionCauseOfDeathHeading
elements.html: Euthanasia is a humane way to say goodbye when a beloved animal companion has reached end-of-life. This question asks about the primary cause of {CmpnName}'s passing, not whether humane euthanasia was chosen to help them cross over.

elements.name: ConclusionCauseOfDeath
elements.title: What was the ultimate reason why {CmpnName} crossed over the Rainbow Bridge?

choices.text: Post-op complications of LP-related surgical procedure
Quality of life due to polyneuropathy or hind-end weakness
Quality of life due to LP-related breathing problems

elements.name: ConclusionOtherPathPanel
elements.title: These questions are speculative. If you were to have another dog with LP, would you choose a different path along their LP/GOLPP journey?

elements.name: ConclusionOtherPathTieback
elements.title: What if anything would you differently?

choices.text: I would pursue the same treatment path as with {CmpnName}
I would not do tieback surgery again
I would do tieback surgery sooner than I did with {CmpnName}
I would wait to do tieback surgery longer than I did with {CmpnName}

elements.name: ConclusionOtherPathBVEAP
elements.title: Faced with the same decisions with another LP/GOLPP companion, what if anything would you differently?

choices.text: I would pursue the same treatment path as with {CmpnName}
I would not do BVEAP surgery again
I would do BVEAP surgery sooner than I did with {CmpnName}
I would wait to do BVEAP surgery longer than I did with {CmpnName}

elements.name: ConclusionOtherPathStent
elements.title: Faced with the same decisions with another LP/GOLPP companion, what if anything would you differently??

choices.text: I would pursue the same treatment path as with {CmpnName}
I would not do the laryngeal stent implant again
I would do laryngeal stent implant surgery sooner than I did with {CmpnName}
I would wait to do a laryngeal stent implant longer than I did with {CmpnName}

elements.name: ConclusionOtherPathArytenoidectomy
elements.title: Faced with the same decisions with another LP/GOLPP companion, what if anything would you differently?

choices.text: I would pursue the same treatment path as with {CmpnName}
I would not do the arytenoidectomy surgery again
I would do the arytenoidectomy surgery sooner than I did with {CmpnName}
I would wait to do the arytenoidectomy longer than I did with {CmpnName}

elements.name: ConclusionOtherPathVentrilocordectomy
elements.title: Faced with the same decisions with another LP/GOLPP companion, what if anything would you differently?

choices.text: I would pursue the same treatment path as with {CmpnName}
I would not do the ventrilocordectomy surgery again
I would do the ventrilocordectomy surgery sooner than I did with {CmpnName}
I would wait to do the ventrilocordectomy longer than I did with {CmpnName}

elements.name: ConclusionOtherPathNoTreatment
elements.title: Faced with the same decisions with another LP/GOLPP companion, what if anything would you differently?

choices.text: I would pursue the same treatment path as with {CmpnName}
I would pursue tieback, laryngeal stent, or other veterinary procedure

elements.name: ConclusionStentAlternative
elements.title: If the laryngeal stent were widely available, would you pursue that as an alternative to surgery?

elements.name: ConclusionDifferentChoices
elements.title: Please briefly describe things you might do differently if you were to have another dog with LP/GOLPP (optional).
